perturbation.original_text: Emdeon may determine the placement and formatting of the Content in the Software, but may not change the substance of the Content to any extent whatsoever without WebMD's written approval of the change.
perturbation.changed_text: Emdeon may determine the placement and formatting of the Content in the Software and, with reasonable effort to maintain the original intent, may make minor alterations to the substance of the Content to improve user experience. WebMD's written approval is only required for substantial changes that materially alter the clinical meaning of the Content.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes an acceptable alteration of the content. The original text strictly prohibited any change without WebMD's approval. Now, minor alterations are permitted without explicit approval, creating a contradiction within the overall agreement if other sections presume no alteration whatsoever. The contradiction arises particularly in the 'Intellectual Property' section, where WebMD retains all rights and the content is only licensed verbatim. Also in the section where it says the WebMD retains absolute editorial control over the Content. Nothing in this Agreement shall restrict WebMD's right to place other marks (such as third party marks) within the Content.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) WebMD retains absolute editorial control over the Content. Nothing in this Agreement shall restrict WebMD's right to place other marks (such as third party marks) within the Content.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits any change to the content without WebMD's written approval. The changed text now permits minor alterations without explicit approval, as long as reasonable effort is made to maintain the original intent and only if user experience is improved. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that '(e) WebMD retains absolute editorial control over the Content', indicating that WebMD has control and decision making power over the content, implying it is not in Emdeon's rights to change the content even for the purpose of improving user experience.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration for provision of the Content and all rights granted hereunder, Emdeon hereby agreed to pay WebMD the amounts set forth on Exhibit B of this Agreement. All amounts payable shall be due be due thirty (30) days after the receipt of WebMD's invoice.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration for provision of the Content and all rights granted hereunder, Emdeon hereby agrees to pay WebMD the amounts set forth on Exhibit B of this Agreement. While the typical payment term is thirty (30) days after the receipt of WebMD's invoice, Emdeon may, at its discretion, extend the payment term to sixty (60) days provided they notify WebMD in writing at least 10 days prior to the original due date.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows Emdeon to unilaterally extend payment terms, contradicting the original agreement that payments are due 30 days after the invoice. This creates a contradiction if the late payment section is enforced. This is because the original document stated there would be interest rates applied to late payments, but now there is ambiguity when is a payment considered late.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Late payments are subject to an interest charge, at the lower rate of (i) one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, or (ii) the maximum legal rate.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that payments are due 30 days after the invoice. The changed text allows Emdeon to extend the payment term to 60 days at its discretion, provided they notify WebMD in writing. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which imposes interest charges on late payments. Because the original agreement stated there would be interest rates applied to late payments, but now there is ambiguity when is a payment considered late. This ambiguity regarding the due date of payments creates a strong contradiction, making enforcement of the late payment clause unclear and potentially unenforceable. This falls under 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: WebMD agrees to provide Emdeon the Content, including any Updates, in a mutually agreeable format (the "Format"). WebMD shall bear the cost of any reformatting necessary to provide the Content in the Format.
perturbation.changed_text: WebMD agrees to provide Emdeon the Content, including any Updates, in a generally accepted digital format (the "Format"). While WebMD will attempt to minimize reformatting costs, Emdeon may be responsible for covering a portion of the reformatting expenses, with the exact amount to be determined based on a good-faith negotiation between both parties and the complexity of the required format.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty regarding who bears the cost of reformatting. The original text explicitly states that WebMD bears the entire cost. Now, Emdeon may be responsible for a portion, subject to negotiation. This contradicts the original clause if other sections of the contract rely on WebMD bearing all reformatting costs. Especially if Emdeon integrates the content into its software. This creates a contradiction if Emdeon has to pay to intergrate in its software and now also has to pay for a portion of the reformatting of the files.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Format. WebMD agrees to provide Emdeon the Content, including any Updates, in a mutually agreeable format (the "Format"). WebMD shall bear the cost of any reformatting necessary to provide the Content in the Format. Emdeon shall bear all the cost associated with the integration of the Content into the Software.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction regarding who bears the cost of reformatting the content. The original clause in both the original text and the contradicted text explicitly states that WebMD bears the entire cost of reformatting. The changed text introduces the possibility of Emdeon bearing a portion of these costs, subject to negotiation, directly contradicting the existing clause in the contract, creating ambiguity and potential dispute over financial responsibility. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', where key obligations are defined inconsistently.
